author: y4rr
content: Done. Three edits: (1) rust-quality forbidden-pattern table now followed by 'Notes on specific patterns' section that codifies the eprintln/println removal-vs-replacement decision (prefer removal when workspace lacks tracing/log AND message is duplicative, decorative, or test-only; prefer Result-return when actionable; only add tracing if workspace already has it). (2) rust-fix-discipline Step 1 now has a 6th bullet for severity reassessment — explicitly invites downgrading 🔴→🟡 or 'no fix needed' when context-reading shows the audit overstated the finding. (3) rust-fix-discipline Step 7 output template now starts with a 'Severity reassessment' line so the reassessment is enforced via the structure, not just narrative.
